attributes.name: commit_message
attributes.value:
feat(commands): add bump --exact

When bumping a prerelease to a new prerelease, honor the detected increment
and preserve the prerelease suffix, rather than bumping to the next
non-prerelease version

attributes.name: commit_message
attributes.value:
refactor: Make tag_format properly default to $version

We've been using this default already in `normalize_tag`,
but setting this value in the settings dict is cleaner.

attributes.name: commit_message
attributes.value:
feat: Introduce tag_regex option with smart default

Closes https://github.com/commitizen-tools/commitizen/issues/519

CLI flag name: --tag-regex

Heavily inspired by
https://github.com/commitizen-tools/commitizen/pull/537, but extends
it with a smart default value to exclude non-release tags. This was
suggested in
https://github.com/commitizen-tools/commitizen/issues/519#issuecomment-1163923719

attributes.name: commit_message
attributes.value:
fix(bump):  only get and validate commits if increment is not provided

This avoids calls to git and additional validations that are not necessary when using --increment

attributes.name: commit_message
attributes.value:
fix(bump.py): `CHANGELOG.md` gets git added and commited correctly

The elements in `version_files` may contain not only a file path, but
also a regex after a colon. Thus make sure to only pass the file path to
 `git add`. Closes #581.

attributes.name: commit_message
attributes.value:
test(changelog): fixes logic issue made evident by latest fix(git) commit

Exception is raised by git error: "fatal: your current branch 'master' does not have any commits yet". This response is more informative than the original "No commits found" exception and the "fail fast" logic is a bit easier to follow.

attributes.name: commit_message
attributes.value:
fix(commit): correct the stage checker before commiting

related to #418

attributes.name: commit_message
attributes.value:
fix(bump): use configured changelog_incremental value when calling `cz bump --changelog`

When using custom templates that include a header for example, then generating the changelog incrementally won't yield the desired result. Therefore the option changelog_incremental should also be honored when using `bump --changelog` to generate the new changelog.

attributes.name: commit_message
attributes.value:
feat(changelog): adds a tag parser to filter tags (--tag-parser; tag_parser)

The git tag parser is used to filter out undesired git tags from changelog. regex default is .* (all).

attributes.name: commit_message
attributes.value:
refactor(changelog): reduces complexity of generate_tree_from_commits

The linter was complaining. Likely best to refactor this further.

attributes.name: commit_message
attributes.value:
feat(bump): let it respect pre-commit reformats when bumping

Fix https://github.com/commitizen-tools/commitizen/issues/502.

Just add `--retry` to `cz bump` and it will attempt to commit twice if the 1st commit fails. Useful if your 1st commit runs a formatter that can for example change the contents of CHANGELOG.md.

attributes.name: commit_message
attributes.value:
test(check): fixes logic issue made evident by the latest fix(git) commit

git was failing with "fatal: ambiguous argument 'master..master': unknown revision or path not in the working tree". git `master` branch doesn't exist unless there is at least one "initial" commit or when only the PR branch has been cloned (e.g. CI).

#572

attributes.name: commit_message
attributes.value:
fix(bump): change --exact-increment to --increment-mode

This provides some future proofing for implementing new version progression behaviors

attributes.name: commit_message
attributes.value:
fix(scm): only search tags that are reachable by the current commit

The scm provider requires tags to be the source of truth for the current version, instead of files.  Therefore, the current version should be the highest tag version upstream from the current commit.

attributes.name: commit_message
attributes.value:
fix: Treat $version the same as unset tag_format in ScmProvider

This should be non-breaking unless users rely on the looser version
validation of TAG_FORMAT_REGEXS compared to their `version_scheme`.

attributes.name: commit_message
attributes.value:
chore(check): add customizable allowed prefixes

The allowed prefixes, which bypass the regex check,
can now be configured.

attributes.name: commit_message
attributes.value:
fix(git): improves git error checking in get_commits

older version of git did not have the --author-date-order argument so it is best to report that kind of error.

attributes.name: commit_message
attributes.value:
refactor(changelog): fixes logic issue made evident by latest fix(git) commit

get_commits was calling git "git log None..None" in the event that a tag range is invalid. Raise exception sooner rather than later.
